title: Loss of taste or smell
summary: Temporary smell reduction is common when the nose is blocked, but sudden marked loss without congestion is more suggestive of COVID-19 or another post-viral smell disorder. Taste complaints are often actually reduced smell.
evidenceSnapshot: Treating nasal obstruction may restore smell when airflow is the problem. Persistent post-viral loss needs medical review; evidence for supplements and home 'cures' is poor.

interventions.name: COVID-19 testing and precautions
interventions.finding: Loss of smell/taste can occur with COVID-19; testing and reducing contact protects others.
interventions.practical: Follow current local testing and isolation advice, especially around vulnerable people.
interventions.mechanism: Identifies a transmissible cause and guides treatment eligibility.
interventions.cautions: A negative test early in illness may need repeating according to local advice.

interventions.name: Saline for blocked nose
interventions.finding: May improve airflow when loss is caused by congestion.
interventions.mechanism: Clears mucus and improves access of odour molecules to receptors.

interventions.name: Smell training for persistent post-viral loss
interventions.finding: Repeated, structured exposure to several distinct odours can support recovery over months.
interventions.practical: Usually involves smelling four familiar odours twice daily for at least 12 weeks; seek clinician guidance.
interventions.mechanism: Encourages olfactory neural recovery and relearning.
interventions.cautions: Do not use irritating chemicals or essential oils inside the nose.

interventions.name: Zinc nasal products
interventions.finding: Intranasal zinc has been associated with loss of smell and should not be used.

practicalSteps: Check whether the nose is physically blocked.
Use smoke alarms and check food spoilage because smell loss reduces hazard detection.
Seek review if loss persists after congestion resolves.
avoidOrCare: Sudden smell loss with neurological symptoms, head injury or severe headache needs urgent assessment.
Do not put garlic, oils or caustic substances inside the nose.

myths.claim: Burnt orange or other viral social-media remedies restore smell.
myths.reality: There is no reliable evidence that a specific food restores olfactory nerves.

myths.claim: Taste and smell loss always means COVID-19.
myths.reality: COVID-19 is one cause; congestion, other viruses, medications, injury and nasal disease can also cause it.

redFlags: New weakness, facial droop, confusion or severe headache
Persistent loss with one-sided blockage, bleeding or weight loss
Inability to smell gas/smoke creating immediate safety risk
unsettled: Optimal smell-training odours, duration and adjunct medicines are still being studied.
